mappings.notes: LLM responses rendered in browser contexts encoded against XSS

mappings.control_name: Verify output encoding in SQL query context
mappings.notes: LLM-generated SQL parameterised — never raw LLM output in SQL context

mappings.control_name: Verify output encoding in OS command context
mappings.notes: LLM-generated commands validated — never raw output in shell context

mappings.control_name: Verify application does not use eval or dynamic code

mappings.control_id: Credentials appearing in model outputs passed to executors
mappings.notes: Credential detection in output pipeline before execution

mappings.control_id: Downstream service credentials with excessive scope
mappings.notes: Apply least-privilege to all credentials used in downstream processing

mappings.control_name: Reuse existing well-secured software — third-party AI model and dataset vetting
mappings.notes: Vet all third-party model weights, datasets, plugins, and libraries before use; verify provenance, licence, and security posture

mappings.control_name: Verify software integrity — model and library supply chain
mappings.notes: Verify integrity of all model artefacts and third-party components using cryptographic signatures and checksums before deployment

mappings.control_name: Archive and protect software releases — model registry
mappings.notes: Maintain a secure, versioned model registry with provenance records for all components; enable auditability and rollback

mappings.control_name: Identify and confirm vulnerabilities — supply chain monitoring
mappings.notes: Monitor for newly disclosed vulnerabilities in third-party AI components; establish a triage process for AI-specific CVEs

mappings.control_name: Supply Chain Risk Management Plan — AI components
mappings.notes: Include all AI components — models, datasets, adapters, libraries, plugins — in the supply chain risk management plan with provenance and risk assessment

mappings.control_name: Supply Chain Controls — model provenance verification
mappings.notes: Implement integrity verification for all AI supply chain components using cryptographic signatures, checksums, and attestation before deployment

mappings.control_name: External Information System Services — third-party AI
mappings.notes: Require third-party AI service providers to meet FedRAMP requirements; establish SLAs covering model security, data handling, and incident notification

mappings.control_name: System Development Life Cycle — AI SDLC
mappings.notes: Integrate AI-specific security activities into the SDLC — model security review, adversarial testing, supply chain verification at each lifecycle phase
Include AI model providers, dataset vendors, and ML library maintainers in third-party ICT risk management; conduct due diligence, contractual oversight, and ongoing monitoring
Identify and classify all AI supply chain components — models, datasets, adapters, libraries, plugins — in the ICT asset inventory with provenance records

mappings.control_name: ICT Risk Management — AI supply chain governance
mappings.notes: Include AI supply chain risk in the ICT risk management framework; define policies for AI component sourcing, vetting, and lifecycle management
Include AI supply chain disruption scenarios in resilience testing; test fallback procedures for third-party AI service failures
